back: Comprehensive documents that provide frameworks for creating consistent, accessible, and user-friendly digital products. They include established patterns, principles, and standards for UI/UX design.

front: What is the purpose of Design Guidelines?
back: To ensure consistency across products, improve usability, accelerate design process, facilitate collaboration, and maintain accessibility standards.
Web Content Accessibility Guidelines - international standards for making web content accessible to people with disabilities. Current version is WCAG 2.1.

front: What are the four WCAG principles (POUR)?
back: Perceivable (content must be presentable in ways users can perceive), Operable (interface must be operable), Understandable (information must be understandable), Robust (content must be robust enough for various technologies).
Google's design language that uses grid-based layouts, responsive animations, padding, and depth effects (shadows) to create visual hierarchy.
Apple's comprehensive guide for designing interfaces across iOS, macOS, watchOS, and tvOS. Emphasizes clarity, deference, and depth.
A collection of reusable components, guided by clear standards, that can be assembled to build any number of applications. Includes style guides, component libraries, and documentation.

front: What is the difference between Style Guide and Design System?
back: Style Guide focuses on visual elements (colors, typography, logos). Design System is more comprehensive, including components, patterns, interactions, and governance.
60% dominant color (backgrounds), 30% secondary color (furniture/elements), 10% accent color (CTAs/highlights) for balanced visual design.
A system of organizing type in different sizes, weights, and colors to establish visual order and guide users through content effectively.

front: What is whitespace in design guidelines?
back: The empty space between design elements. It improves readability, creates visual breathing room, establishes relationships between elements, and enhances aesthetics.

Visibility of system status, match between system and real world, user control and freedom, consistency and standards, error prevention, recognition rather than recall, flexibility and efficiency of use, aesthetic and minimalist design, help users recognize/fix errors, help and documentation.
A spacing system where all design elements align to multiples of 8px. Used for consistent layouts across different screen sizes and densities.

front: What is color contrast ratio requirement?
back: WCAG requires minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio for normal text and 3:1 for large text (18px+ or 14px+ bold). AAA level requires 7:1 for normal text.
The time to acquire a target is a function of the distance to and size of the target. Larger, closer targets are easier to click/tap.
